content:
# Mark Chapter 12 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), folios 38v–39r (PDF pages 77–78) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_012_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_012_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Witness:** Codex Vaticanus (B, 03) — consulted via [DigiVatLib](https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209)

---

¹ And he-began to-speak to-them in parables: "A-man planted a-vineyard and placed a-fence around [it] and dug a-wine-vat and built a-tower and leased it to-tenant-farmers and went-abroad.

² And he-sent to the tenant-farmers at-the-season a-servant, in-order-that from the tenant-farmers he-might-receive from the fruit of-the vineyard.

³ And having-taken him, they-beat [him] and sent-[him]-away empty.

⁴ And again he-sent to them another servant; and that-one they-struck-on-the-head and dishonored.

⁵ And another he-sent, and that-one they-killed; and many others — some they-beat and some they-killed.

⁶ He-had yet one, a-beloved son. He-sent him last to them, saying, 'They-will-respect my son.'

⁷ But those tenant-farmers said to themselves, 'This is the heir. Come, let-us-kill him, and the inheritance will-be ours.'

⁸ And having-taken him, they-killed [him] and threw him outside the vineyard.

⁹ What will the lord of-the vineyard do? He-will-come and will-destroy the tenant-farmers and will-give the vineyard to-others.

¹⁰ Have-you not read this scripture: 'The stone which the builders rejected, this became [the] head of-[the] corner.

¹¹ From [the] Lord this came-about, and it-is marvelous in our eyes'?"

¹² And they-were-seeking to-seize him, and they-feared the crowd, for they-knew that he-spoke the parable against them. And having-left him, they-went-away.

¹³ And they-send to him some of-the Pharisees and of-the Herodians, in-order-that they-might-trap him in-a-word.

¹⁴ And having-come, they-say to-him, "Teacher, we-know that you-are truthful and it-does not matter to-you about anyone, for you-do not look at [the] face of-men, but in truth you-teach the way of-God. Is-it-permitted to-give tribute to Caesar or not? Should-we-give or should-we not give?"

¹⁵ But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said to-them, "Why do-you-test me? Bring me a-denarius, that I-may-see [it]."

¹⁶ And they-brought [one]. And he-says to-them, "Whose [is] this image and the inscription?" And they-said to-him, "Caesar's."

¹⁷ And Jesus said to-them, "The-[things] of-Caesar give-back to-Caesar, and the-[things] of-God to-God." And they-were-marveling at him.

¹⁸ And Sadducees come to him, who say [there] is no resurrection, and they-were-asking him, saying,

¹⁹ "Teacher, Moses wrote for-us that if someone's brother dies and leaves-behind a-wife and does not leave a-child, that his brother should-take the wife and raise-up offspring for-his brother.

²⁰ There-were seven brothers. And the first took a-wife and dying did not leave offspring.

²¹ And the second took her and died, not leaving offspring. And the third likewise.

²² And the seven did not leave offspring. Last of-all, the woman also died.

²³ In the resurrection, when they-rise, whose wife will-she-be? For the seven had her [as] wife."

²⁴ Jesus said to-them, "Is-this not why you-err — [because] you-know neither the scriptures nor the power of-God?

²⁵ For when they-rise from [the] dead, they neither marry nor are-given-in-marriage, but are as angels in the heavens.

²⁶ But concerning the dead, that they-are-raised, have-you not read in the book of Moses, at the bush, how God spoke to-him, saying, 'I [am] the God of Abraham and the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob'?

²⁷ He-is not God of-[the] dead but of-[the] living. You-err greatly."

²⁸ And one of-the scribes, having-come, having-heard them disputing, knowing that he-answered them well, asked him, "Which commandment is first of-all?"

²⁹ Jesus answered, "The first is: 'Hear, O Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one.

³⁰ And you-shall-love the Lord your God from your whole heart and from your whole soul and from your whole mind and from your whole strength.'

³¹ The second [is] this: 'You-shall-love your neighbor as yourself.' There-is no other commandment greater than these."

³² And the scribe said to-him, "Well, Teacher, in truth you-said that he-is one and there-is no other besides him;

³³ and to-love him from [the] whole heart and from [the] whole understanding and from [the] whole strength, and to-love the neighbor as oneself, is much-more-than all the whole-burnt-offerings and sacrifices."

³⁴ And Jesus, having-seen that he-answered wisely, said to-him, "You-are not far from the kingdom of God." And no-one any-longer dared to-question him.

³⁵ And answering, Jesus was-saying, teaching in the temple, "How do the scribes say that the Christ is son of-David?

³⁶ David himself said in the Holy Spirit, 'The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right until I-place your enemies beneath your feet.'

³⁷ David himself calls him 'lord' — and from-where is-he his son?" And the great crowd was-hearing him gladly.

³⁸ And in his teaching he-was-saying, "Beware of the scribes, those wanting to-walk-around in robes and [wanting] greetings in the marketplaces

³⁹ and first-seats in the synagogues and first-places at the dinners,

⁴⁰ those devouring the houses of-widows and for-a-pretense praying long [prayers]. These will-receive greater condemnation."

⁴¹ And having-sat-down opposite the treasury, he-was-observing how the crowd throws copper-coins into the treasury. And many rich-[people] were-throwing much.

⁴² And one poor widow, having-come, threw two small-coins, which is a-quadrans.

⁴³ And having-called his disciples, he-said to-them, "Truly I-say to-you that this poor widow threw more than all those throwing into the treasury.

⁴⁴ For all threw from their surplus, but she from her lack threw all whatever she-had — her whole livelihood."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **12:1 — Vineyard parable:** The description echoes Isaiah 5:1-7, Israel as God's vineyard. The "beloved son" (ΥΙΟΝ ΑΓΑΠΗΤΟΝ) echoes the baptism (1:11) and transfiguration (9:7). Sinaiticus preserves the full parable clearly.

2. **12:10-11 — Psalm 118:22-23 quotation:** The rejected stone becoming the cornerstone. In the LXX tradition, this psalm was associated with the feast of Tabernacles and the entrance liturgy.

3. **12:17 — ΑΠΟΔΟΤΕ ("give back"):** Not simply "give" (δίδωμι) but "give back" (ἀποδίδωμι) — what already belongs to Caesar, return to him. The verb implies that the coin, bearing Caesar's image, already belongs to Caesar.

4. **12:26 — "at the bush" (ΕΠΙ ΤΟΥ ΒΑΤΟΥ):** This is a method of citing Scripture by reference to the section heading, since chapters and verses did not yet exist. "At the bush" = "in the passage about the burning bush."

5. **12:29 — The Shema:** Jesus quotes Deuteronomy 6:4-5 — the central confession of Jewish faith. Only Mark includes the full Shema ("Hear, O Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one") in this exchange. Matthew and Luke abbreviate.

6. **12:42 — "two small coins, which is a quadrans":** Mark translates the Greek λεπτά into the Roman quadrans for his Gentile audience — further evidence of a Roman audience.

— EMET, AI Translator
